back_translation: Paul, apostle of Jesus Christ according to the will of God, according to the promise of life that is in Jesus Christ
To Timothy, beloved child: may grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and from Jesus Christ our Lord be with you
I thank God, whom I serve with a pure conscience as did my forefathers, as I constantly remember you in my prayers, both night and day
I greatly long to see you, remembering your tears, so that I may be filled with joy
I am reminded of the sincere faith in you, which dwelt first in your grandmother, namely Lois, and in your mother, namely Eunice, and I am confident it is in you also
For this reason, I remind you to fan into renewed flame the gift of God that is in you. That gift came through the laying on of my hands
For God has not given us a spirit of cowardice, but a spirit of power, love, and self-control
Therefore do not be ashamed of the testimony concerning our Lord, or of me who am a prisoner for his sake, but suffer hardship together for the gospel by the power of God
He saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which he gave to us in Jesus Christ from before the beginning of eternal ages
Now that grace has been manifested through the appearing of Jesus Christ, our Savior, who abolished death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel
For this very gospel, I was appointed to be a proclaimer, an apostle, and a teacher
For this reason I endure these sufferings, but I am not ashamed, for I know the one whom I have believed, and I am confident that he is able to guard the deposit until that day
Hold to the pattern of correct words that you heard from me, with faith and love that are in Jesus Christ
Guard the good deposit, by means of the Holy Spirit who dwells in us
You already know this, that everyone in the province of Asia turned their back on me, among them are Phygelus and Hermogenes
May the Lord grant mercy to the family of Onesiphorus, for he refreshed me many times and was not ashamed of my chains
On the contrary, when he arrived in the city of Rome, he diligently sought me until he found me
May the Lord graciously grant him to find mercy from the Lord in that day. And you yourself know better than anyone how much he served at the city of Ephesus
